```{r}
    summarise(residual = mean(residual, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
    mutate(residual = pmax(pmin(residual, 4), -4))

  ggplot(plot_summary, aes(x = year, y = bin, fill = residual)) +
    geom_raster(interpolate = FALSE) +
    facet_wrap(~ Fishery, ncol = 2, scales = "free_y") +
    scale_fill_opal_resid() +
    labs(x = "Year", y = y_label) +
    theme_opal()
}
```

```{r}
#| echo: false
#| label: fig-bet-lf-composition-residuals
#| fig-cap: "Length-composition OSA residual diagnostics for fishery 12 in the fitted WCPO bigeye tuna diagnostic model. Colours show the mean OSA residual in each year-length cell, with blue indicating negative residuals and red indicating positive residuals."
#| fig-height: 4.8
#| fig-width: 8

plot_bet_comp_residuals(
  filter(bet_comp_residuals, type == "Length composition", Fishery == "Fishery 12"),
  y_label = "Length (cm)"
)
```

```{r}
#| echo: false
#| label: fig-bet-wf-composition-residuals
#| fig-cap: "Weight-composition OSA residual diagnostics for fishery 15 in the fitted WCPO bigeye tuna diagnostic model. Colours show the mean OSA residual in each year-weight cell, with blue indicating negative residuals and red indicating positive residuals."
#| fig-height: 4.8
#| fig-width: 8

plot_bet_comp_residuals(
  filter(bet_comp_residuals, type == "Weight composition", Fishery == "Fishery 15"),
  y_label = "Weight (kg)"
)
```

```{r}
#| echo: false
#| label: fig-bet-composition-residual-qq
#| fig-cap: "Normal Q-Q diagnostics for length- and weight-composition OSA residuals in the fitted WCPO bigeye tuna diagnostic model, shown separately by composition type and fishery. The grey line is the fixed standard-normal one-to-one reference; it is not fitted separately within panels."
#| fig-height: 8
#| fig-width: 8

bet_comp_qq <- bet_comp_residuals %>%
  mutate(
    fishery_number = as.integer(sub("Fishery ", "", as.character(Fishery))),
    sdnr = ave(residual, type, Fishery, FUN = sd),
    panel = sprintf("%s\n%s\nSDNR = %.2f", type, Fishery, sdnr)
  ) %>%
  group_by(type, Fishery) %>%
  arrange(residual, .by_group = TRUE) %>%
  mutate(theoretical = qnorm(ppoints(n()))) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  arrange(type, fishery_number) %>%
  mutate(panel = factor(panel, levels = unique(panel)))

bet_comp_qq_limit <- max(abs(c(bet_comp_qq$theoretical, bet_comp_qq$residual)), na.rm = TRUE)
bet_comp_qq_limit <- ceiling(min(max(bet_comp_qq_limit, 4), 5.5) * 2) / 2

ggplot(bet_comp_qq, aes(x = theoretical, y = residual)) +
  geom_abline(intercept = 0, slope = 1, colour = opal_cols["struct"],
              linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.35) +
  geom_point(size = 0.28, alpha = 0.25) +
  facet_wrap(~ panel, ncol = 4) +
  coord_equal(xlim = c(-bet_comp_qq_limit, bet_comp_qq_limit),
              ylim = c(-bet_comp_qq_limit, bet_comp_qq_limit)) +
  labs(x = "Theoretical quantiles", y = "Sample quantiles") +
  theme_opal() +
  theme(strip.text = element_text(size = 6.5))
```

```{r}
#| echo: false
#| label: fig-bet-agg6-composition-residual-qq
#| fig-cap: "Normal Q-Q diagnostics for grouped length- and weight-composition OSA residuals in the matched six-fishery WCPO bigeye tuna aggregation sensitivity. These panels repeat the layout of @fig-bet-composition-residual-qq using finite bin-level `compResidual::resMulti()` residuals from the grouped observed and predicted compositions; the grey line is the fixed standard-normal one-to-one reference."
#| fig-height: 4.8
#| fig-width: 8

bet_agg6_comp_qq <- bet_agg6_comp_residual_rows %>%
  filter(is.finite(residual)) %>%
  mutate(
    type = factor(type, levels = c("Length composition", "Weight composition")),
    sdnr = ave(residual, type, group_label, FUN = sd),
    panel = sprintf("%s\n%s\nSDNR = %.2f", type, group_label, sdnr)
  ) %>%
  group_by(type, group_label) %>%
  arrange(residual, .by_group = TRUE) %>%
  mutate(theoretical = qnorm(ppoints(n()))) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  arrange(type, fishery_num) %>%
  mutate(panel = factor(panel, levels = unique(panel)))

bet_agg6_comp_qq_limit <- max(
  abs(c(bet_agg6_comp_qq$theoretical, bet_agg6_comp_qq$residual)),
  na.rm = TRUE
)
bet_agg6_comp_qq_limit <- ceiling(min(max(bet_agg6_comp_qq_limit, 4), 5.5) * 2) / 2

ggplot(bet_agg6_comp_qq, aes(x = theoretical, y = residual)) +
  geom_abline(intercept = 0, slope = 1, colour = opal_cols["struct"],
              linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.35) +
  geom_point(size = 0.28, alpha = 0.25) +
  facet_wrap(~ panel, ncol = 3) +
  coord_equal(xlim = c(-bet_agg6_comp_qq_limit, bet_agg6_comp_qq_limit),
              ylim = c(-bet_agg6_comp_qq_limit, bet_agg6_comp_qq_limit)) +
  labs(x = "Theoretical quantiles", y = "Sample quantiles") +
  theme_opal() +
  theme(strip.text = element_text(size = 6.5))
```

```{r}
#| echo: false
#| label: fig-bet-recruitment-deviations
#| fig-cap: "Estimated recruitment deviations for the fitted WCPO bigeye tuna diagnostic model and matched six-fishery aggregation sensitivity, shown by seasonal index month."
#| fig-height: 5
#| fig-width: 8

bet_rec_raw <- bet$diagnostics$recruitment_raw
bet_rec_lookup <- bet_rec_raw$cpue_lookup %>%
  arrange(ts)
bet_rec_months <- sort(unique(bet_rec_lookup$month))
bet_rec_season_lookup <- tibble(
  month = bet_rec_months,
  season = seq_along(bet_rec_months),
  season_label = paste0(month.name[month])
)
bet_rec_df <- bet_rec_lookup %>%
  left_join(bet_rec_season_lookup, by = "month") %>%
  mutate(
    rdev = bet_rec_raw$rdev_y,
    estimated = if (length(bet_rec_raw$map_rdev) == length(bet_rec_raw$rdev_y)) {
      !is.na(bet_rec_raw$map_rdev)
    } else {
      TRUE
    },
    model = "opal",
    season_label = factor(season_label, levels = month.name[bet_rec_months])
  )

bet_agg6_rec_lookup <- bet_agg6_raw$result$fit$data$cpue_data %>%
  distinct(ts, year, month) %>%
  arrange(ts)
bet_agg6_rec_df <- bet_agg6_rec_lookup %>%
  left_join(bet_rec_season_lookup, by = "month") %>%
  mutate(
    rdev = as.numeric(bet_agg6_raw$result$par_list$rdev_y),
    estimated = if (length(bet_agg6_raw$result$fit$map$rdev_y) == length(rdev)) {
      !is.na(bet_agg6_raw$result$fit$map$rdev_y)
    } else {
      TRUE
    },
    model = "6-fishery opal",
    season_label = factor(season_label, levels = month.name[bet_rec_months])
  )

bet_rec_plot_df <- bind_rows(bet_rec_df, bet_agg6_rec_df) %>%
  mutate(model = factor(model, levels = c("opal", "6-fishery opal")))

ggplot(bet_rec_plot_df, aes(x = year, y = rdev, colour = model)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linewidth = 0.3, colour = "grey40") +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.45) +
  geom_point(size = 0.9, alpha = 0.75) +
  facet_wrap(~ season_label, ncol = 2) +
  scale_colour_opal(
    breaks = c("opal", "6-fishery opal"),
    labels = c("15-fishery opal", "6-fishery opal")
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = scales::breaks_pretty(n = 6)) +
  labs(x = "Year", y = "Recruitment deviation", colour = "Model") +
  theme_opal()
```

```{r}
#| echo: false
#| label: fig-bet-selectivity-length
#| fig-cap: "Selectivity-at-length curves for WCPO bigeye tuna fisheries. Teal lines show the fitted opal selectivity curves; dashed navy lines show the SS3 reference selectivity curves used to initialise the opal model; dotted coral lines show the MFCL reference selectivity curves."
#| fig-height: 8
#| fig-width: 8

sel_logistic_plain <- function(len, par) {
  mu <- mean(len)
  sd_len <- sd(len)
  a50 <- mu + par[1] * sd_len
  w95 <- exp(par[2]) * sd_len
  1 / (1 + exp(-log(19) * (len - a50) / w95))
}

sel_double_normal_plain <- function(len, par) {
  mu <- mean(len)
  sd_len <- sd(len)
  peak <- mu + par[1] * sd_len
  upselex <- exp(par[3]) * sd_len^2
  downselex <- exp(par[4]) * sd_len^2
  point1 <- 1 / (1 + exp(-par[5]))
  point2 <- 1 / (1 + exp(-par[6]))
  j2 <- length(len)
  bin_width <- len[2] - len[1]
  peak2 <- peak + bin_width + (0.99 * len[j2] - peak - bin_width) /
    (1 + exp(-par[2]))
  t1min <- exp(-(len[1] - peak)^2 / upselex)
  t2min <- exp(-(len[j2] - peak2)^2 / downselex)
  t1 <- len - peak
  t2 <- len - peak2
  join1 <- 1 / (1 + exp(-(20 / (1 + abs(t1))) * t1))
  join2 <- 1 / (1 + exp(-(20 / (1 + abs(t2))) * t2))
  asc <- point1 + (1 - point1) * (exp(-t1^2 / upselex) - t1min) / (1 - t1min)
  dsc <- 1 + (point2 - 1) * (exp(-t2^2 / downselex) - 1) / (t2min - 1)
  asc * (1 - join1) + join1 * (1 - join2 + dsc * join2)
}

bet_selectivity_curve <- function(par_sel, series) {
  map_dfr(seq_len(nrow(par_sel)), function(f) {
    sel <- if (bet_sel_raw$sel_type_f[f] == 1L) {
      sel_logistic_plain(bet_sel_raw$len_mid, par_sel[f, ])
    } else {
      sel_double_normal_plain(bet_sel_raw$len_mid, par_sel[f, ])
    }
    tibble(
      Fishery = paste("Fishery", f),
      length = bet_sel_raw$len_mid,
      selectivity = as.numeric(sel),
      series = series
    )
  })
}

# MFCL reference selectivity — already realized at-length, not parametric,
# so reshape rather than evaluate through a selectivity function
wcpo_bet_xmodel_selex <- readRDS(file.path("cached", "bet", "wcpo_bet_xmodel_selex.rds"))

n_fishery <- nrow(bet_sel_raw$par_est)

# simpler/cleaner version of the above filter — keep fisheries that exist
# in the opal comparison
mfcl_selectivity_df <- wcpo_bet_xmodel_selex %>%
  filter(Sex == 0, Yr == max(Yr), Fleet <= n_fishery) %>%
  transmute(
    Fishery = paste("Fishery", Fleet),
    length = variable,
    selectivity = value,
    series = "MFCL"
  )

bet_selectivity_df <- bind_rows(
  bet_selectivity_curve(bet_sel_raw$par_est, "opal"),
  bet_selectivity_curve(bet_sel_raw$par_ref, "SS3"),
  mfcl_selectivity_df
) %>%
  mutate(
    Fishery = factor(Fishery, levels = paste("Fishery", seq_len(n_fishery))),
    series = factor(series, levels = c("SS3", "MFCL", "opal"))
  )

ggplot(bet_selectivity_df, aes(x = length, y = selectivity)) +
  geom_line(data = filter(bet_selectivity_df, series == "SS3"),
            colour = opal_models["SS3"], linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.55) +
  geom_line(data = filter(bet_selectivity_df, series == "MFCL"),
            colour = opal_models["MFCL"], linetype = "dotted", linewidth = 0.55) +
  geom_line(data = filter(bet_selectivity_df, series == "opal"),
            colour = opal_models["opal"], linewidth = 0.7) +
  facet_wrap(~ Fishery, ncol = 3) +
  labs(x = "Length (cm)", y = "Selectivity") +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0, 1)) +
  theme_opal()
```

```{r}
#| echo: false
#| label: fig-bet-agg6-selectivity
#| fig-cap: "Estimated selectivity-at-length curves for the six-fishery WCPO bigeye tuna aggregation sensitivity. Facet labels identify the original BET fisheries pooled into each aggregation group."
#| fig-height: 4.6
#| fig-width: 8

bet_agg6_selectivity_plot <- bet_agg6$selectivity %>%
  left_join(bet_agg6_group_labels, by = "fishery") %>%
  mutate(group_label = factor(group_label, levels = bet_agg6_group_labels$group_label))

ggplot(bet_agg6_selectivity_plot, aes(x = length, y = selectivity)) +
  geom_line(colour = opal_models["6-fishery opal"], linewidth = 0.85) +
  facet_wrap(~ group_label, ncol = 3) +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0, 1)) +
  labs(x = "Length (cm)", y = "Selectivity") +
  theme_opal(base_size = 9) +
  theme(strip.text = element_text(size = 7))
```

```{r}
#| echo: false
#| label: fig-bet-biomass
#| fig-cap: "Estimated spawning biomass and relative biomass trajectories for the fitted WCPO bigeye tuna diagnostic model and the matched six-fishery aggregation sensitivity. Shaded ribbons show approximate 95% intervals from model-based standard errors; the retained `opal` ribbon uses `RTMB::sdreport()` with a supplied positive-definite fixed-effect Hessian. The `opal` models are compared with the SS3 and MFCL reference models; external-model SB/SB0 uses the same median SSB/depletion reference scale reported in the text."
#| fig-height: 5
#| fig-width: 8

bet_biomass_unc <- bet$diagnostics$biomass_uncertainty
if (is.null(bet_biomass_unc)) {
  stop("BET biomass uncertainty is not available in the cached results")
}
bet_biomass <- bind_rows(
  bet_biomass_unc %>%
    transmute(
      year,
      quantity = "Spawning~biomass~(mt)",
      value = spawning_biomass,
      lower = if_else(is.finite(spawning_biomass_se),
                      pmax(0, spawning_biomass - 1.96 * spawning_biomass_se),
                      NA_real_),
      upper = if_else(is.finite(spawning_biomass_se),
                      spawning_biomass + 1.96 * spawning_biomass_se,
                      NA_real_)
    ),
  bet_biomass_unc %>%
    transmute(
      year,
      quantity = "SB[t]/SB[0]",
      value = sb_sb0,
      lower = if_else(is.finite(sb_sb0_se),
                      pmax(0, sb_sb0 - 1.96 * sb_sb0_se),
                      NA_real_),
      upper = if_else(is.finite(sb_sb0_se),
                      sb_sb0 + 1.96 * sb_sb0_se,
                      NA_real_)
    ),
  bet_biomass_unc %>%
    transmute(
      year,
      quantity = "SB[t]/SB[list(t,F==0)]",
      value = dynamic_depletion,
      lower = if_else(is.finite(dynamic_depletion_se),
                      pmax(0, dynamic_depletion - 1.96 * dynamic_depletion_se),
                      NA_real_),
      upper = if_else(is.finite(dynamic_depletion_se),
                      dynamic_depletion + 1.96 * dynamic_depletion_se,
                      NA_real_)
    )
) %>%
  mutate(
    quantity = factor(
      quantity,
      levels = c("Spawning~biomass~(mt)", "SB[t]/SB[0]", "SB[t]/SB[list(t,F==0)]")
    )
  )

bet_biomass$model = "opal"
bet_agg6_biomass <- bind_rows(
  bet_agg6$biomass %>%
    transmute(year, quantity = "Spawning~biomass~(mt)",
              value = spawning_biomass, lower = NA_real_, upper = NA_real_,
              model = "6-fishery opal"),
  bet_agg6$biomass %>%
    transmute(year, quantity = "SB[t]/SB[0]",
              value = sb_sb0, lower = NA_real_, upper = NA_real_,
              model = "6-fishery opal"),
  bet_agg6$biomass %>%
    transmute(year, quantity = "SB[t]/SB[list(t,F==0)]",
              value = dynamic_depletion, lower = NA_real_, upper = NA_real_,
              model = "6-fishery opal")
) %>%
  mutate(quantity = factor(quantity, levels = levels(bet_biomass$quantity)))

# load ss3 and mfcl trajectories for comparison
bet_xmodel <- readRDS(file.path("cached", "bet", "wcpo_bet_xmodel_biomass.rds"))

xmodel_b0_for_plot <- bet_xmodel_b0 %>%
  filter(model %in% c("MFCL-v11", "02-fix-sel")) %>%
  mutate(model = recode(model, "MFCL-v11" = "MFCL", "02-fix-sel" = "SS3")) %>%
  select(model, B0)
if (nrow(xmodel_b0_for_plot) != 2L || any(!is.finite(xmodel_b0_for_plot$B0))) {
  stop("External-model B0 scale is unavailable for the BET biomass comparison")
}

xmodel_long <- bet_xmodel %>%
  filter(model %in% c("MFCL-v11", "02-fix-sel")) %>%
  mutate(model = recode(model, "MFCL-v11" = "MFCL", "02-fix-sel" = "SS3")) %>%
  arrange(model, ts) %>%
  left_join(xmodel_b0_for_plot, by = "model") %>%
  mutate(static_depletion = ssb / B0,
         static_depletion_se = ssb_se / B0) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(ssb, depletion, static_depletion),
    names_to = "quantity_id",
    values_to = "value"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    se = case_when(
      quantity_id == "ssb"               ~ ssb_se,
      quantity_id == "depletion"         ~ depletion_se,
      quantity_id == "static_depletion"  ~ static_depletion_se
    ),
    quantity = case_when(
      quantity_id == "ssb"               ~ "Spawning~biomass~(mt)",
      quantity_id == "static_depletion"  ~ "SB[t]/SB[0]",
      quantity_id == "depletion"         ~ "SB[t]/SB[list(t,F==0)]"
    ),
    # MFCL is annual -> use year as-is; SS3 is quarterly -> add season fraction
    year = if_else(model == "MFCL", year, year + (season - 1) / 4),
    lower = pmax(0, value - 1.96 * se),
    upper = value + 1.96 * se,
    quantity = factor(quantity, levels = levels(bet_biomass$quantity))
  ) %>%
  select(year, quantity, value, lower, upper, model)

bet_combined <- bind_rows(bet_biomass, bet_agg6_biomass, xmodel_long) %>%
  filter(year <= 2018)

ggplot(bet_combined, aes(x = year, y = value, colour = model, fill = model)) +
  geom_ribbon(data = filter(bet_combined, is.finite(lower), is.finite(upper)),
              aes(ymin = lower, ymax = upper),
              alpha = 0.18, colour = NA) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.9) +
  facet_wrap(~ quantity, nrow = 1, scales = "free_y", labeller = label_parsed) +
  scale_colour_opal(breaks = c("opal", "6-fishery opal", "SS3", "MFCL")) +
  scale_fill_opal(breaks = c("opal", "6-fishery opal", "SS3", "MFCL")) +
  labs(x = "Year", y = NULL, colour = "Model", fill = "Model") +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, NA), expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.05))) +
  theme_opal() +
  guides(fill = "none")
```
